player_utterance: John, I've been sitting with a question that won't leave me alone — do you have a moment to think it through together?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, clicking a pen against his thumb without noticing

player_utterance: It's about the man who died — I keep turning it over and can't seem to put it down.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, weight shifted to one foot, looking past the player toward the lit windows of the house

player_utterance: I didn't know him at all — that's part of what keeps pulling at me, that I'm this troubled by a stranger.

believer_state_after.posture: lowering himself onto the bench, gesturing for the player to sit if they want

player_utterance: The pastor called him a pillar of the community — those were the words used this morning, weren't they?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning back, looking up at the darkening sky

player_utterance: He also said "we know where he is now" — and I noticed the room went quiet in a particular way when those words landed.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward now, elbows on knees, staring at his folded hands

player_utterance: What does your own tradition actually teach about a man who ends his life — because I don't think the pastor said anything about that this morning.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still, hands flat on his knees, not looking at the player

voice_offers.read: He noticed the silence too; press the doctrine beneath it.
voice_offers.line: You teach this history — what does your confession actually say about a man who dies by his own hand?

voice_offers.read: He's leaned forward, hands folded; let the room hold him.

voice_response.matched_line: You teach this history — what does your confession actually say about a man who dies by his own hand?

player_utterance: You don't have to answer it right now — I only noticed that the pastor seemed to be speaking carefully around it rather than about it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting back slightly, looking at the player with something like gratitude

voice_offers.read: He's avoiding the doctrine he teaches for a living.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Augsburg Confession. Don't go vague on me now — what does it say?

voice_offers.read: His stillness is the answer; pushing now wastes it.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer that here. I just noticed the pastor wouldn't.

voice_response.matched_line: You don't have to answer that here. I just noticed the pastor wouldn't.

player_utterance: The pastor meant well, I think — but there's a word he wouldn't let himself say, and I wonder if you can say it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward, hands clasped tight, looking at the ground between his feet

voice_offers.read: He softened; gratitude opens more than pressure now.
voice_offers.line: It was a kind thing he tried to say. I think he meant it.

voice_offers.read: Gratitude is the opening — name what the pastor avoided.
voice_offers.line: The word is suicide, John. The pastor wouldn't say it. Will you?

player_utterance: There's no performance required here — just the word, whenever you're ready, in your own voice.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward, hands clasped, the pen left on the bench where he set it

voice_offers.read: He's hiding behind the pastor's silence. Name it.
voice_offers.line: Suicide. That's the word. Say it out loud, John — once, in your own voice.

voice_offers.read: His hands are white-knuckled; pushing now will shut him.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not in a hurry.

voice_response.matched_line: Suicide. That's the word. Say it out loud, John — once, in your own voice.

player_utterance: You set the pen down — I noticed that, and it matters.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting back slowly, hands open and resting palms-up on his knees, looking at the player

voice_offers.read: He's stalling behind silence; the word is right there.
voice_offers.line: Suicide, John. One word. You've circled it three times — say it or tell me why you can't.

voice_offers.read: He set the pen down. Something in him is already answering.
voice_offers.line: You put the pen down. That's something. I saw it.

voice_response.matched_line: You put the pen down. That's something. I saw it.

player_utterance: Whose name belongs beside that word you just said?

believer_state_after.posture: palms still open on his knees, but he closes them now into loose fists and then opens them again, looking at them as if they belong to someone else

voice_offers.read: His hands are open; the room is doing the work.
He's softened but still hasn't said it; one quiet push.
voice_offers.line: Whose name goes with the word, John?

voice_response.matched_line: Whose name goes with the word, John?

player_utterance: Whoever he was — you don't have to make a ceremony of it, just the name, when you're ready to carry it in your mouth.

believer_state_after.posture: hands still open on his knees, head bowed slightly, eyes closed for a moment before he speaks

voice_offers.read: He's at the threshold; the name will finish what the word began.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. You owe him that much — out loud, here, now.

voice_offers.read: His hands don't know themselves; pushing now could shatter him.
voice_offers.line: Whoever he is — you don't have to perform it. Just the name, when your mouth can hold it.

voice_response.matched_line: Whoever he is — you don't have to perform it. Just the name, when your mouth can hold it.

player_utterance: That name has been carved over your head for fourteen months, and now it's sitting in the open air between us.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still, hands resting on his knees, eyes on the candle

voice_offers.read: He is almost there; pressure now would shatter the threshold.
Three silences in. Silence has become its own evasion.
voice_offers.line: John. The name. Now — before you talk yourself out of it again.
